understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move a pushable object (block) onto the goal tile(s) marked by '+' in the semantic grid. Success for each level was achieved when the objective, such as placing a block on the goal, was met as reported by a WIN message.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was performed by directing the currently selected piece in one of four directions. Some objects could be pushed if adjacent, and movement could be blocked by walls or grid boundaries. There could be multiple selectable pieces, and switching control between them was possible by using CLICK on their pixel coordinates. Walls displayed different blocking behaviors, while the semantic grid provided a symbolic overview of the level. The move budget limited the number of actions per level, requiring efficient planning.

understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was performed by directing the currently selected piece in one of four directions. Some objects could be pushed if adjacent, and movement could be blocked by walls or grid boundaries.
